Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%searchInput%') for full-text search is inefficient as it cannot use indexes and performs poorly on large datasets. Consider using a dedicated full-text search engine like Elasticsearch, or database-native full-text search features (e.g., PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery or MySQL's FULLTEXT indexes).

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the MEMBER table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the columns needed for MemberDto, e.g., `dsl.select(MEMBER.ID, MEMBER.NAME, ...)`
